mn38:0.2: The Greater Discourse on the Destruction of *Taṇhā*
mn38:1.2: at one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Sāvatthī, in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s park.
mn38:10.1: “When one doubts, *bhikkhus*, ‘Has this come to be or not?’ perplexity arises?”
mn38:10.3: “When one doubts, *bhikkhus*, ‘Has that arisen from its nutriment or not?’ perplexity arises?”
mn38:10.5: “When one doubts, *bhikkhus*, ‘With the cessation of that nutriment, is what has come to be subject to cessation or not?’ perplexity arises?”
mn38:11.1: “When one sees, *bhikkhus*, with right discernment [*sammappaññāya*] as it truly is, ‘This has come to be,’ whatever perplexity there is, it is abandoned?”
mn38:11.3: “When one sees, *bhikkhus*, with right discernment [*sammappaññāya*] as it truly is, ‘That has arisen from its nutriment,’ whatever perplexity there is, it is abandoned?”
mn38:11.5: “When one sees, *bhikkhus*, with right discernment [*sammappaññāya*] as it truly is, ‘With the cessation of that nutriment, what has come to be is subject to cessation,’ whatever perplexity there is, it is abandoned?”
mn38:12.1: “So here, *bhikkhus*, you are free from perplexity in this way too: ‘This has come to be’?”
mn38:12.3: “So here, *bhikkhus*, you are free from perplexity in this way too: ‘That has arisen from its nutriment’?”
mn38:12.5: “So here, *bhikkhus*, you are free from perplexity in this way too: ‘With the cessation of that nutriment, what has come to be is subject to cessation’?”
mn38:13.1: “Has this been well seen by you, *bhikkhus*, with right discernment [*sammappaññāya*] as it truly is: ‘This has come to be’?”
mn38:13.3: “Has this been well seen by you, *bhikkhus*, with right discernment [*sammappaññāya*] as it truly is: ‘That has arisen from its nutriment’?”
mn38:13.5: “Has this been well seen by you, *bhikkhus*, with right discernment [*sammappaññāya*] as it truly is: ‘With the cessation of that nutriment, what has come to be is subject to cessation’?”
mn38:14.1: “If, *bhikkhus*, you clung to this view, so purified and so bright, cherished it, treasured it, and treated it as your own, would you understand the *dhamma* taught by me as comparable to a raft, for crossing over and not for grasping?”
mn38:14.3: “If, *bhikkhus*, you did not cling to this view, so purified and so bright, did not cherish it, did not treasure it, and did not treat it as your own, would you understand the *dhamma* taught by me as comparable to a raft, for crossing over and not for grasping?”
mn38:15.1: “There are these four nutriments, *bhikkhus*, for the maintenance of beings that have come to be, or for the support of those seeking birth.
mn38:15.3: Edible food, coarse or subtle; contact as the second; mental volition as the third; and *viññāṇa* as the fourth.
mn38:16.1: And these four nutriments, *bhikkhus*, have what as their source, what as their origin, what as their birth, what as their production?
mn38:16.10: The dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] has *nāmarūpa* as its source, *nāmarūpa* as its origin, *nāmarūpa* as its birth, *nāmarūpa* as its production.
mn38:16.11: And this *nāmarūpa*, *bhikkhus*, has what as its source, what as its origin, what as its birth, what as its production?
mn38:16.12: *Nāmarūpa* has *viññāṇa* as its source, *viññāṇa* as its origin, *viññāṇa* as its birth, *viññāṇa* as its production.
mn38:16.13: And this *viññāṇa*, *bhikkhus*, has what as its source, what as its origin, what as its birth, what as its production?
mn38:16.14: *Viññāṇa* has *saṅkhāras* as its source, *saṅkhāras* as its origin, *saṅkhāras* as its birth, *saṅkhāras* as its production.
mn38:16.15: And these *saṅkhāras*, *bhikkhus*, have what as their source, what as their origin, what as their birth, what as their production?
mn38:16.16: *Saṅkhāras* have blindness [*avijjā*] as their source, blindness [*avijjā*] as their origin, blindness [*avijjā*] as their birth, blindness [*avijjā*] as their production.
mn38:16.2: These four nutriments have *taṇhā* as their source, *taṇhā* as their origin, *taṇhā* as their birth, *taṇhā* as their production.
mn38:16.3: And this *taṇhā*, *bhikkhus*, has what as its source, what as its origin, what as its birth, what as its production?
mn38:16.4: *Taṇhā* has *vedanā* as its source, *vedanā* as its origin, *vedanā* as its birth, *vedanā* as its production.
mn38:16.5: And this *vedanā*, *bhikkhus*, has what as its source, what as its origin, what as its birth, what as its production?
mn38:16.6: *Vedanā* has contact as its source, contact as its origin, contact as its birth, contact as its production.
mn38:16.7: And this contact, *bhikkhus*, has what as its source, what as its origin, what as its birth, what as its production?
mn38:16.8: Contact has the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as its source, the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as its origin, the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as its birth, the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as its production.
mn38:16.9: And this dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], *bhikkhus*, has what as its source, what as its origin, what as its birth, what as its production?
mn38:17.11: with birth as condition, aging-and-death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, distress, and anguish come to be.
mn38:17.12: Such is the origin of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
mn38:17.4: with *nāmarūpa* as condition, the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
mn38:17.5: with the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as condition, contact;
mn38:18.1: ‘With birth as condition, aging-and-death’: so it has been said;
mn38:18.11: “‘With *upādāna* as condition, *bhava*’: so it has been said;
mn38:18.12: but is *bhava* with *upādāna* as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.14: so it is for us here—
mn38:18.16: “‘With *taṇhā* as condition, *upādāna*’: so it has been said; but is *upādāna* with *taṇhā* as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.18: so it is for us here—
mn38:18.2: but is aging-and-death with birth as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.20: “‘With *vedanā* as condition, *taṇhā*’: so it has been said;
mn38:18.21: but is *taṇhā* with *vedanā* as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.23: so it is for us here—
mn38:18.25: “‘With contact as condition, *vedanā*’: so it has been said;
mn38:18.26: but is *vedanā* with contact as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.28: so it is for us here—
mn38:18.30: “‘With the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as condition, contact’: so it has been said;
mn38:18.31: but is contact with the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.32: “With the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as condition, *bhante*, contact;
mn38:18.33: so it is for us here—
mn38:18.34: with the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as condition, contact.”
mn38:18.35: “‘With *nāmarūpa* as condition, the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*]’: so it has been said;
mn38:18.36: but is the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] with *nāmarūpa* as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.37: “With *nāmarūpa* as condition, *bhante*, the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
mn38:18.38: so it is for us here—
mn38:18.39: with *nāmarūpa* as condition, the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*].”
mn38:18.4: so it is for us here—
mn38:18.40: “‘With *viññāṇa* as condition, *nāmarūpa*’: so it has been said;
mn38:18.41: but is *nāmarūpa* with *viññāṇa* as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.43: so it is for us here—
mn38:18.45: “‘With *saṅkhāras* as condition, *viññāṇa*’: so it has been said;
mn38:18.46: but is *viññāṇa* with *saṅkhāras* as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.48: so it is for us here—
mn38:18.50: “‘With blindness [*avijjā*] as condition, *saṅkhāras*’: so it has been said;
mn38:18.51: but are *saṅkhāras* with blindness [*avijjā*] as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.53: so it is for us here—
mn38:18.6: “‘With *bhava* as condition, birth’: so it has been said;
mn38:18.7: but is birth with *bhava* as condition, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:18.9: so it is for us here—
mn38:19.14: with birth as condition, aging-and-death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, distress, and anguish come to be.
mn38:19.15: Such is the origin of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
mn38:19.2: Thus, *bhikkhus*, you too speak in this way, and I too speak in this way—
mn38:19.3: when this exists, this comes to be; with the arising of this, this arises, namely:
mn38:19.7: with *nāmarūpa* as condition, the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
mn38:19.8: with the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] as condition, contact;
mn38:2.1: Now at that time an evil view had arisen in a *bhikkhu* named Sāti, the fisherman’s son, as follows:
mn38:2.2: “As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another.”
mn38:20.1: But with the remainderless fading away and cessation of blindness [*avijjā*], cessation of *saṅkhāras*;
mn38:20.10: with the cessation of *bhava*, cessation of birth;
mn38:20.11: with the cessation of birth, aging-and-death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, distress, and anguish cease.
mn38:20.12: Such is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
mn38:20.2: with the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, cessation of *viññāṇa*;
mn38:20.3: with the cessation of *viññāṇa*, cessation of *nāmarūpa*;
mn38:20.4: with the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
mn38:20.5: with the cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], cessation of contact;
mn38:20.6: with the cessation of contact, cessation of *vedanā*;
mn38:20.7: with the cessation of *vedanā*, cessation of *taṇhā*;
mn38:20.8: with the cessation of *taṇhā*, cessation of *upādāna*;
mn38:20.9: with the cessation of *upādāna*, cessation of *bhava*;
mn38:21.1: ‘With the cessation of birth, cessation of aging-and-death’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.10: with the cessation of *bhava*, cessation of birth.”
mn38:21.11: “‘With the cessation of *upādāna*, cessation of *bhava*’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.12: but is there cessation of *bhava* with the cessation of *upādāna*, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.14: so it is for us here—
mn38:21.15: with the cessation of *upādāna*, cessation of *bhava*.”
mn38:21.16: “‘With the cessation of *taṇhā*, cessation of *upādāna*’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.17: but is there cessation of *upādāna* with the cessation of *taṇhā*, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.19: so it is for us here—
mn38:21.2: but is there cessation of aging-and-death with the cessation of birth, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.20: with the cessation of *taṇhā*, cessation of *upādāna*.”
mn38:21.21: “‘With the cessation of *vedanā*, cessation of *taṇhā*’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.22: but is there cessation of *taṇhā* with the cessation of *vedanā*, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.24: so it is for us here—
mn38:21.25: with the cessation of *vedanā*, cessation of *taṇhā*.”
mn38:21.26: “‘With the cessation of contact, cessation of *vedanā*’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.27: but is there cessation of *vedanā* with the cessation of contact, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.28: “With the cessation of contact, *bhante*, cessation of *vedanā*;
mn38:21.29: so it is for us here—
mn38:21.3: “With the cessation of birth, *bhante*, cessation of aging-and-death;
mn38:21.30: with the cessation of contact, cessation of *vedanā*.”
mn38:21.31: “‘With the cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], cessation of contact’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.32: but is there cessation of contact with the cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.33: “With the cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], *bhante*, cessation of contact;
mn38:21.34: so it is for us here—
mn38:21.35: with the cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], cessation of contact.”
mn38:21.36: “‘With the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*]’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.37: but is there cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*] with the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.38: “With the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, *bhante*, cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
mn38:21.39: so it is for us here—
mn38:21.4: so it is for us here—
mn38:21.40: with the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*].”
mn38:21.41: “‘With the cessation of *viññāṇa*, cessation of *nāmarūpa*’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.42: but is there cessation of *nāmarūpa* with the cessation of *viññāṇa*, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.44: so it is for us here—
mn38:21.45: with the cessation of *viññāṇa*, cessation of *nāmarūpa*.”
mn38:21.46: “‘With the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, cessation of *viññāṇa*’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.47: but is there cessation of *viññāṇa* with the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.49: so it is for us here—
mn38:21.5: with the cessation of birth, cessation of aging-and-death.”
mn38:21.50: with the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, cessation of *viññāṇa*.”
mn38:21.51: “‘With the cessation of blindness [*avijjā*], cessation of *saṅkhāras*’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.52: but is there cessation of *saṅkhāras* with the cessation of blindness [*avijjā*], *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.53: “With the cessation of blindness [*avijjā*], *bhante*, cessation of *saṅkhāras*;
mn38:21.54: so it is for us here—
mn38:21.55: with the cessation of blindness [*avijjā*], cessation of *saṅkhāras*.”
mn38:21.6: “‘With the cessation of *bhava*, cessation of birth’: so it has been said;
mn38:21.7: but is there cessation of birth with the cessation of *bhava*, *bhikkhus*, or not, or how is it here?”
mn38:21.8: “With the cessation of *bhava*, *bhante*, cessation of birth;
mn38:21.9: so it is for us here—
mn38:22.10: with the cessation of *vedanā*, cessation of *taṇhā*;
mn38:22.11: with the cessation of *taṇhā*, cessation of *upādāna*;
mn38:22.12: with the cessation of *upādāna*, cessation of *bhava*;
mn38:22.13: with the cessation of *bhava*, cessation of birth;
mn38:22.14: with the cessation of birth, aging-and-death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, distress, and anguish cease.
mn38:22.15: Such is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
mn38:22.2: Thus, *bhikkhus*, you too speak in this way, and I too speak in this way—
mn38:22.3: when this does not exist, this does not come to be; with the cessation of this, this ceases, namely:
mn38:22.4: with the cessation of blindness [*avijjā*], cessation of *saṅkhāras*;
mn38:22.5: with the cessation of *saṅkhāras*, cessation of *viññāṇa*;
mn38:22.6: with the cessation of *viññāṇa*, cessation of *nāmarūpa*;
mn38:22.7: with the cessation of *nāmarūpa*, cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*];
mn38:22.8: with the cessation of the dimension of the six senses [*saḷāyatana*], cessation of contact;
mn38:22.9: with the cessation of contact, cessation of *vedanā*;
mn38:23.1: Would you, *bhikkhus*, knowing and seeing in this way, run back to the past thus:
mn38:23.2: ‘Were we in the past? Were we not in the past? What were we in the past? How were we in the past? Having been what, what were we in the past?’”
mn38:23.4: “Would you, *bhikkhus*, knowing and seeing in this way, run forward to the future thus:
mn38:23.5: ‘Will we be in the future? Will we not be in the future? What will we be in the future? How will we be in the future? Having been what, what will we be in the future?’”
mn38:24.1: “Would you, *bhikkhus*, knowing and seeing in this way, be inwardly perplexed about the present thus:
mn38:24.10: “Would you, *bhikkhus*, knowing and seeing in this way, point to another teacher?”
mn38:24.12: “Would you, *bhikkhus*, knowing and seeing in this way, return to those observances, ceremonies, and auspicious signs of the many ascetics and brahmins as essential?”
mn38:24.14: “Is it not, *bhikkhus*, that you speak only what has been known by yourselves, seen by yourselves, and understood by yourselves?”
mn38:24.2: ‘Am I? Am I not? What am I? How am I? From where has this being come? Where will it be going?’”
mn38:24.4: “Would you, *bhikkhus*, knowing and seeing in this way, speak thus:
mn38:24.5: ‘Our Teacher is respected by us, and we speak in this way out of respect for the Teacher’?”
mn38:24.7: “Would you, *bhikkhus*, knowing and seeing in this way, speak thus:
mn38:24.8: ‘The ascetic says this, and since we are ascetics we speak in this way’?”
mn38:25.1: “*Sādhu*, *bhikkhus*. You have been led on by me with this *dhamma* visible here and now, timeless, inviting inspection, onward-leading, to be personally known by the wise.
mn38:25.2: This *dhamma*, *bhikkhus*, is visible here and now, timeless, inviting inspection, onward-leading, to be personally known by the wise—
mn38:25.3: when that is said, it is said in dependence on this.
mn38:26.1: Now, *bhikkhus*, the descent into the womb occurs through the coming together of three things.
mn38:26.2: Here the mother and father have come together, but the mother is not in season, and the gandhabba is not present; so there is not yet a descent into the womb.
mn38:26.3: Here the mother and father have come together, and the mother is in season, but the gandhabba is not present; so there is not yet a descent into the womb.
mn38:26.4: But when, *bhikkhus*, the mother and father have come together, the mother is in season, and the gandhabba is present—through the coming together of these three things, there is a descent into the womb.
mn38:27.1: Then, *bhikkhus*, for nine or ten months the mother carries that heavy burden in her womb with great anxiety.
mn38:27.2: Then, *bhikkhus*, after nine or ten months have passed, the mother gives birth to that heavy burden with great anxiety.
mn38:27.3: Then, when that child has been born, she nourishes him with her own blood.
mn38:27.4: For this, *bhikkhus*, is blood in the *vinaya* of the noble one: mother’s milk.
mn38:28.1: Then, *bhikkhus*, as that boy grows and his *indriyas* mature,
mn38:28.2: he plays with the toys of little boys, such as toy ploughs, stick games, somersaults, windmills, toy measures, toy carts, and toy bows.
mn38:29.1: Then, *bhikkhus*, as that boy grows and his *indriyas* mature,
mn38:29.2: he enjoys himself supplied and endowed with the five cords of sensual pleasure:
mn38:29.3: forms [*rūpehi*] knowable by the eye that are wished for, desired, agreeable, pleasing in form [*piyarūpehi*], connected with sensual desire, and enticing;
mn38:29.7: tangibles knowable by the body that are wished for, desired, agreeable, pleasing in form [*piyarūpehi*], connected with sensual desire, and enticing.
mn38:3.10: In many ways, friend Sāti, *viññāṇa* has been said by the *Bhagavā* to be *paṭiccasamuppāda*'ed [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*]; apart from conditions, there is no arising of *viññāṇa*.”
mn38:3.11: Yet though the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, was questioned, pressed, and spoken to by those *bhikkhus* in this way, he still obstinately held tight to that same evil view and declared it:
mn38:3.12: “Exactly so, friends. As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another.”
mn38:3.2: “It seems an evil view has arisen in a *bhikkhu* named Sāti, the fisherman’s son:
mn38:3.3: ‘As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another.’”
mn38:3.4: Then those *bhikkhus* approached the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son; having approached, they said to the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son:
mn38:3.5: “Is it true, friend Sāti, that such an evil view has arisen in you:
mn38:3.6: ‘As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another’?”
mn38:3.7: “Exactly so, friends. As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another.”
mn38:3.8: Then those *bhikkhus*, wishing to detach the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, from that evil view, questioned him, pressed him, and spoke to him:
mn38:3.9: “Do not say so, friend Sāti. Do not misrepresent the *Bhagavā*. It is not *sādhu* to misrepresent the *Bhagavā*, for the *Bhagavā* would not speak like this.
mn38:30.1: Seeing a form [*rūpaṁ*] with the eye, he is attracted to pleasing forms [*rūpe*], and repelled by displeasing forms [*rūpe*]; and he dwells with *sati* of the body unestablished, with a limited mind [*cetaso*].
mn38:30.10: touching a tangible with the body …
mn38:30.11: knowing a *dhamma* with the mind, he is attracted to pleasing *dhammas*, and repelled by displeasing *dhammas*; and he dwells with *sati* of the body unestablished, with a limited mind [*cetaso*].
mn38:30.12: He does not understand as it truly is that liberation of mind [*cetovimuttiṁ*] and liberation by discernment [*paññāvimuttiṁ*] where those evil unskillful *dhammas* cease without remainder.
mn38:30.13: Having thus entered into attraction and opposition, whatever *vedanā* he feels—whether *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*—he delights in that *vedanā*, welcomes it, and remains holding to it.
mn38:30.14: As he delights in that *vedanā*, welcomes it, and remains holding to it, enjoyment arises.
mn38:30.15: Whatever enjoyment there is in *vedanās*, that is *upādāna*; with that *upādāna* as condition, *bhava*; with *bhava* as condition, birth; with birth as condition, aging-and-death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, distress, and anguish come to be.
mn38:30.16: Such is the origin of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
mn38:30.2: He does not understand as it truly is that liberation of mind [*cetovimuttiṁ*] and liberation by discernment [*paññāvimuttiṁ*] where those evil unskillful *dhammas* cease without remainder.
mn38:30.3: Having thus entered into attraction and opposition, whatever *vedanā* he feels—whether *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*—he delights in that *vedanā*, welcomes it, and remains holding to it.
mn38:30.4: As he delights in that *vedanā*, welcomes it, and remains holding to it, enjoyment arises.
mn38:30.5: Whatever enjoyment there is in *vedanās*, that is *upādāna*; with that *upādāna* as condition, *bhava*; with *bhava* as condition, birth; with birth as condition, aging-and-death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, distress, and anguish come to be.
mn38:30.6: Such is the origin of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
mn38:30.7: Hearing a sound with the ear …
mn38:30.8: smelling an odor with the nose …
mn38:30.9: tasting a taste with the tongue …
mn38:31.1: Here, *bhikkhus*, a Tathāgata arises in the world, an *arahant*, fully awakened, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, well-gone, knower of the world, unsurpassed trainer of persons to be tamed, teacher of *devatās* and humans, awakened one, the *Bhagavā*.
mn38:31.2: Having realized by his own direct knowledge this world with its *devatās*, Māras, and Brahmās, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its *devatās* and humans, he makes it known.
mn38:31.3: He teaches the *dhamma* that is good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, with meaning and phrasing;
mn38:31.4: he reveals the holy life that is entirely complete and purified.
mn38:32.1: A householder or householder’s son, or one reborn in some family, hears that *dhamma*.
mn38:32.2: Having heard that *dhamma*, he gains faith in the Tathāgata.
mn38:32.3: Endowed with that gain of faith, he reflects thus:
mn38:32.4: ‘Household life is crowded, a dusty path; going forth is open air.
mn38:32.5: It is not easy, while living at home, to live the holy life wholly complete, wholly pure, polished like a conch shell.
mn38:32.6: What if I shaved off hair and beard, put on ochre robes, and went forth from home into homelessness?’
mn38:32.7: Then at a later time, having given up a small mass of wealth or a great mass of wealth, having given up a small circle of relatives or a great circle of relatives, he shaves off hair and beard, puts on ochre robes, and goes forth from home into homelessness.
mn38:33.1: Having thus gone forth and undertaken the training and way of life of the *bhikkhus*, abandoning the killing of living beings, he abstains from killing living beings; with rod and weapon laid aside, conscientious and compassionate, he dwells caring for the welfare of all living beings.
mn38:33.10: He abstains from dancing, singing, music, and shows,
mn38:33.11: abstains from wearing garlands, perfumes, and cosmetics, and from adornment and beautification,
mn38:33.12: abstains from high and luxurious beds,
mn38:33.13: abstains from accepting gold and silver,
mn38:33.16: abstains from accepting women and girls,
mn38:33.17: abstains from accepting female and male bondservants,
mn38:33.18: abstains from accepting goats and sheep,
mn38:33.19: abstains from accepting chickens and pigs,
mn38:33.2: Abandoning the taking of what is not given, he abstains from taking what is not given; he takes what is given, expects what is given, and dwells with himself pure by not stealing.
mn38:33.20: abstains from accepting elephants, cattle, horses, and mares,
mn38:33.21: abstains from accepting fields and land,
mn38:33.22: abstains from running messages and errands,
mn38:33.24: abstains from false weights, false metals, and false measures,
mn38:33.25: abstains from bribery, deception, fraud, and crookedness,
mn38:33.26: abstains from mutilating, killing, imprisoning, highway robbery, plunder, and violence.
mn38:33.3: Abandoning unchastity, he is celibate, living apart, abstaining from the sexual act, the village practice.
mn38:33.4: Abandoning false speech, he abstains from false speech; he speaks truth, is bound to truth, firm, reliable, and no deceiver of the world.
mn38:33.5: Abandoning divisive speech, he abstains from divisive speech: having heard something here, he does not repeat it elsewhere to divide these people; having heard something elsewhere, he does not repeat it here to divide those people. Thus he is one who reconciles the divided, supports the united, delights in harmony, enjoys harmony, rejoices in harmony, and speaks words that make for harmony.
mn38:33.6: Abandoning harsh speech, he abstains from harsh speech; he speaks such words as are gentle, pleasing to the ear, lovable, heart-touching, courteous, desired by many, and agreeable to many.
mn38:33.7: Abandoning idle chatter, he abstains from idle chatter; he speaks at the proper time, speaks what is factual, speaks what is beneficial, speaks *dhamma*, speaks *vinaya*; at the proper time he speaks words worth treasuring, reasoned, measured, and connected with benefit.
mn38:33.8: He abstains from damaging seeds and plant life,
mn38:33.9: eats one meal a day, abstaining at night and abstaining from eating at the wrong time.
mn38:34.1: He is content with a robe to protect the body and alms food to sustain the belly. Wherever he goes, he goes taking only these with him.
mn38:34.2: Just as a bird, wherever it flies, flies with its wings as its only burden;
mn38:34.3: so too a *bhikkhu* is content with a robe to protect the body and alms food to sustain the belly. Wherever he goes, he goes taking only these with him.
mn38:34.4: Endowed with this noble aggregate of ethical conduct, he experiences inward blameless *sukha*.
mn38:35.1: Seeing a form [*rūpaṁ*] with the eye, he is not one who grasps at the *nimitta*, nor one who grasps at secondary features.
mn38:35.2: Since, if he were to dwell with the eye *indriya* unrestrained, covetousness and distress, evil unskillful *dhammas*, might flow in upon him, he practices restraint of it; he guards the eye *indriya* and undertakes restraint in the eye *indriya*.
mn38:35.3: Hearing a sound with the ear …
mn38:35.4: smelling an odor with the nose …
mn38:35.5: tasting a taste with the tongue …
mn38:35.6: touching a tangible with the body …
mn38:35.7: knowing a *dhamma* with the mind, he is not one who grasps at the *nimitta*, nor one who grasps at secondary features.
mn38:35.8: Since, if he were to dwell with the mind *indriya* unrestrained, covetousness and distress, evil unskillful *dhammas*, might flow in upon him, he practices restraint of it; he guards the mind *indriya* and undertakes restraint in the mind *indriya*.
mn38:35.9: Endowed with this noble restraint of the *indriyas*, he experiences inward unblemished *sukha*.
mn38:36.1: He acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when going forward and returning; he acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when looking ahead and looking aside; he acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when bending and stretching; he acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when carrying his outer robe, bowl, and robes; he acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when eating, drinking, chewing, and tasting; he acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when urinating and defecating; he acts with full awareness [*sampajānakārī*] when walking, standing, sitting, sleeping, waking, speaking, and keeping silent.
mn38:37.1: Endowed with this noble aggregate of ethical conduct, endowed with this noble contentment, endowed with this noble restraint of the *indriyas*, endowed with this noble *sati* and full awareness [*satisampajaññena*],
mn38:37.2: he resorts to a secluded [*vivittaṁ*] dwelling place: a wilderness, the root of a tree, a mountain, a ravine, a mountain cave, a charnel ground, a forest thicket, the open air, or a heap of straw.
mn38:38.1: After his meal, when he has returned from almsround, he sits down, folding his legs crosswise, setting his body straight, and establishing *sati* before him.
mn38:38.2: Abandoning covetousness for the world, he dwells with a mind [*cetasā*] free of covetousness; he purifies the mind [*cittaṁ*] of covetousness.
mn38:38.3: Abandoning ill will and hostility, he dwells with a mind free of ill will, caring for the welfare of all living beings; he purifies the mind [*cittaṁ*] of ill will and hostility.
mn38:38.4: Abandoning dullness and drowsiness, he dwells free of dullness and drowsiness, perceiving light, mindful and fully aware [*sampajāno*]; he purifies the mind [*cittaṁ*] of dullness and drowsiness.
mn38:38.5: Abandoning restlessness and remorse, he dwells unagitated, with a mind [*citto*] inwardly peaceful; he purifies the mind [*cittaṁ*] of restlessness and remorse.
mn38:38.6: Abandoning perplexity, he dwells having crossed beyond perplexity, without uncertainty about skillful *dhammas*; he purifies the mind [*cittaṁ*] of perplexity.
mn38:39.1: Having abandoned these five hindrances, defilements of the mind [*cetaso*] that weaken discernment [*paññāya*],
mn38:39.2: quite secluded [*vivicceva*] from sensual pleasures, secluded [*vivicca*] from unskillful *dhammas*, he enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*, which has thought [*savitakkaṁ*] and examination [*savicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*vivekajaṁ*].
mn38:39.3: Again, *bhikkhus*, with the stilling of thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], a *bhikkhu* enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*, with inner settling, unification of the mind [*cetaso ekodibhāvaṁ*], without thought [*avitakkaṁ*], without examination [*avicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* …
mn38:39.5: he enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*.
mn38:4.1: Since those *bhikkhus* were unable to detach the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, from that evil view, they approached the *Bhagavā*; having approached, they paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, those *bhikkhus* said to the *Bhagavā*:
mn38:4.10: ‘Exactly so, friends. As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another.’
mn38:4.11: Then, *bhante*, wishing to detach the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, from that evil view, we questioned him, pressed him, and spoke to him:
mn38:4.12: ‘Do not say so, friend Sāti. Do not misrepresent the *Bhagavā*. It is not *sādhu* to misrepresent the *Bhagavā*, for the *Bhagavā* would not speak like this.
mn38:4.13: In many ways, friend Sāti, *viññāṇa* has been said by the *Bhagavā* to be *paṭiccasamuppāda*'ed [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*]; apart from conditions, there is no arising of *viññāṇa*.’
mn38:4.14: Yet though the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, was questioned, pressed, and spoken to by us in this way, *bhante*, he still obstinately held tight to that same evil view and declared it:
mn38:4.15: ‘Exactly so, friends. As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another.’
mn38:4.16: Since, *bhante*, we were unable to detach the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, from that evil view, we report this matter to the *Bhagavā*.”
mn38:4.2: “*Bhante*, an evil view has arisen in a *bhikkhu* named Sāti, the fisherman’s son:
mn38:4.3: ‘As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another.’
mn38:4.4: We heard, *bhante*, that an evil view had arisen in a *bhikkhu* named Sāti, the fisherman’s son:
mn38:4.5: ‘As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another.’
mn38:4.6: Then, *bhante*, we approached the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son; having approached, we said to the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son:
mn38:4.7: ‘Is it true, friend Sāti, that such an evil view has arisen in you:
mn38:4.8: “As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another”?’
mn38:4.9: When this was said, *bhante*, the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, said to us:
mn38:40.1: Seeing a form [*rūpaṁ*] with the eye, he is not attracted to pleasing forms [*rūpe*], and not repelled by displeasing forms [*rūpe*]; and he dwells with *sati* of the body established, with an immeasurable mind [*cetaso*].
mn38:40.2: He understands as it truly is that liberation of mind [*cetovimuttiṁ*] and liberation by discernment [*paññāvimuttiṁ*] where those evil unskillful *dhammas* cease without remainder.
mn38:40.3: Having thus abandoned attraction and opposition, whatever *vedanā* he feels—whether *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*—he does not delight in that *vedanā*, does not welcome it, and does not remain holding to it.
mn38:40.4: As he does not delight in that *vedanā*, does not welcome it, and does not remain holding to it, whatever enjoyment there is in *vedanās* ceases.
mn38:40.5: With the cessation of his enjoyment, cessation of *upādāna*; with the cessation of *upādāna*, cessation of *bhava*; with the cessation of *bhava*, cessation of birth; with the cessation of birth, aging-and-death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, distress, and anguish cease.
mn38:40.6: Such is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
mn38:41.1: Hearing a sound with the ear …
mn38:41.10: Such is the cessation of this whole mass of *dukkha*.
mn38:41.11: Remember this, *bhikkhus*, as my brief teaching on liberation through the destruction of *taṇhā*; but remember the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, as caught in the great net of *taṇhā*, bound in the mass of *taṇhā*.”
mn38:41.13: Satisfied, those *bhikkhus* delighted in the *Bhagavā*’s words.
mn38:41.14: The Greater Discourse on the Destruction of *Taṇhā* is finished, the eighth.
mn38:41.2: smelling an odor with the nose …
mn38:41.3: tasting a taste with the tongue …
mn38:41.4: touching a tangible with the body …
mn38:41.5: knowing a *dhamma* with the mind, he is not attracted to pleasing *dhammas*, and not repelled by displeasing *dhammas*; and he dwells with *sati* of the body established, with an immeasurable mind [*cetaso*],
mn38:41.6: he understands as it truly is that liberation of mind [*cetovimuttiṁ*] and liberation by discernment [*paññāvimuttiṁ*] where those evil unskillful *dhammas* cease without remainder.
mn38:41.7: Having thus abandoned attraction and opposition, whatever *vedanā* he feels—whether *sukha* or *dukkha* or neither-*dukkha*-nor-*sukha*—he does not delight in that *vedanā*, does not welcome it, and does not remain holding to it.
mn38:41.8: As he does not delight in that *vedanā*, does not welcome it, and does not remain holding to it, whatever enjoyment there is in *vedanās* ceases.
mn38:41.9: With the cessation of his enjoyment, cessation of *upādāna*; with the cessation of *upādāna*, cessation of *bhava*; with the cessation of *bhava*, cessation of birth; with the cessation of birth, aging-and-death, sorrow, lamentation, *dukkha*, distress, and anguish cease.
mn38:5.11: “It is this, *bhante*, that speaks and feels, that experiences here and there the result of good and bad *kammas*.”
mn38:5.12: “Misguided man, to whom have you ever known me to teach the *dhamma* in that way?
mn38:5.13: Misguided man, have I not said in many ways that *viññāṇa* is *paṭiccasamuppāda*'ed [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*], that apart from conditions there is no arising of *viññāṇa*?
mn38:5.14: Yet you, misguided man, by your own bad grasp, both misrepresent us and dig yourself up, and you generate much demerit.
mn38:5.15: For that will lead to your harm and *dukkha* for a long time, misguided man.”
mn38:5.2: “Come, *bhikkhu*, in my name call the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son:
mn38:5.4: “Yes, *bhante*,” that *bhikkhu* replied to the *Bhagavā*, and he approached the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son; having approached, he said to the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son:
mn38:5.6: “Yes, friend,” the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, replied to that *bhikkhu*, and he approached the *Bhagavā*; having approached, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, the *Bhagavā* said to the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son:
mn38:5.7: “Is it true, Sāti, that such an evil view has arisen in you:
mn38:5.8: ‘As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another’?”
mn38:5.9: “Exactly so, *bhante*. As I understand the *dhamma* taught by the *Bhagavā*, it is this same *viññāṇa* that runs on and wanders through *saṃsāra*, not another.”
mn38:6.3: has this *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, even warmed up in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*]?”
mn38:6.6: When this was said, the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, sat silent, dismayed, shoulders drooping, head down, brooding, without a reply.
mn38:6.7: Then the *Bhagavā*, knowing that the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, was silent, dismayed, shoulders drooping, head down, brooding, without a reply, said to the *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son:
mn38:6.8: “You will be known, misguided man, by this evil view of your own.
mn38:7.2: “Do you too, *bhikkhus*, understand the *dhamma* taught by me in the same way as this *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, who by his own bad grasp both misrepresents us and digs himself up, and generates much demerit?”
mn38:7.4: For in many ways, *bhante*, *viññāṇa* has been said by the *Bhagavā* to be *paṭiccasamuppāda*'ed [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*]; apart from conditions, there is no arising of *viññāṇa*.”
mn38:7.6: It is *sādhu*, *bhikkhus*, that you understand the *dhamma* taught by me in this way.
mn38:7.7: For in many ways, *bhikkhus*, *viññāṇa* has been said by me to be *paṭiccasamuppāda*'ed [*paṭiccasamuppannaṁ*]; apart from conditions, there is no arising of *viññāṇa*.
mn38:7.8: Yet this *bhikkhu* Sāti, the fisherman’s son, by his own bad grasp both misrepresents us and digs himself up, and generates much demerit.
mn38:7.9: For that will lead to that misguided man’s harm and *dukkha* for a long time.
mn38:8.1: “Whatever condition, *bhikkhus*, *viññāṇa* arises dependent on, it is reckoned by that very condition.
mn38:8.10: a fire that burns dependent on sticks is reckoned simply as a stick-fire;
mn38:8.11: a fire that burns dependent on grass is reckoned simply as a grass-fire;
mn38:8.12: a fire that burns dependent on cow dung is reckoned simply as a cow-dung-fire;
mn38:8.13: a fire that burns dependent on chaff is reckoned simply as a chaff-fire;
mn38:8.14: a fire that burns dependent on rubbish is reckoned simply as a rubbish-fire.
mn38:8.15: So too, *bhikkhus*, whatever condition *viññāṇa* arises dependent on, it is reckoned by that very condition.
mn38:8.16: Dependent on the eye and forms [*rūpe*], *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as eye-*viññāṇa*;
mn38:8.17: dependent on the ear and sounds, *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as ear-*viññāṇa*,
mn38:8.18: dependent on the nose and odors, *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as nose-*viññāṇa*,
mn38:8.19: dependent on the tongue and tastes, *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as tongue-*viññāṇa*.
mn38:8.2: Dependent on the eye and forms [*rūpe*], *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as eye-*viññāṇa*;
mn38:8.20: Dependent on the body and tangibles, *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as body-*viññāṇa*.
mn38:8.21: Dependent on the mind and *dhammas*, *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as mind-*viññāṇa*.
mn38:8.3: dependent on the ear and sounds, *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as ear-*viññāṇa*;
mn38:8.4: dependent on the nose and odors, *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as nose-*viññāṇa*;
mn38:8.5: dependent on the tongue and tastes, *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as tongue-*viññāṇa*;
mn38:8.6: dependent on the body and tangibles, *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as body-*viññāṇa*;
mn38:8.7: dependent on the mind and *dhammas*, *viññāṇa* arises, and it is reckoned simply as mind-*viññāṇa*.
mn38:8.8: Just as, *bhikkhus*, whatever condition a fire burns dependent on, it is reckoned by that very condition.
mn38:8.9: A fire that burns dependent on wood is reckoned simply as a wood-fire;
mn38:9.1: “Do you see, *bhikkhus*, ‘This has come to be’?”
mn38:9.3: “Do you see, *bhikkhus*, ‘That has arisen from its nutriment’?”
mn38:9.5: “Do you see, *bhikkhus*, ‘With the cessation of that nutriment, what has come to be is subject to cessation’?”
